ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PUNITIVE DAMAGES CONTINUED FROM NOV-16-2010 TO ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION AT NOV-18-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 220. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. SPAIN AND PUERTO RICO'S LAWS CONTROL ON THE ISSUE OF AVAILABILITY OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES BECAUSE THE ALLEGED EXPOSURES TO DEFENDANT'S ASBESTOS CEMENT PIPE OCCURRED IN SPAIN AND PUERTO RICO. CALIFORNIA'S INTEREST, IF ANY, IN HAVING ITS PUNITIVE DAMAGES LAWS APPLIED TO THIS CASE AS TO DEFENDANT IS VERY ATTENUATED AND MUST GIVE WAY TO THE MORE SUBSTANTIAL INTEREST OF SPAIN AND PUERTO RICO IN HAVING THEIR LAWS APPLY TO EXPOSURES THAT OCCURRED IN THEIR JURISDICTIONS. THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO THURSDAY NOVEMBER 18, 2010 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 220. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Continued To Nov-18-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 220
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant CertainTeed Corporation's motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is granted. Spain and Puerto Rico's laws control on the issue of availability of punitive damages because the alleged exposures to defendant's asbestos cement pipe occurred in Spain and Puerto Rico. California's interest, if any, in having its punitive damages laws applied to this case as to defendant is very attenuated and must give way to the more substantial interest of Spain and Puerto Rico in having their laws apply to exposures that occurred in their jurisdictions. The hearing on this motion is continued on the court's own motion to Thursday November 18, 2010 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
